visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.documents_required: Please click here for the checklist for Business
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Business Visa]
Please note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange       fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of Euro 31 per application over and above visa fees (all applicable taxes included).
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
Processing time of a short stay visa  depends on the purpose and duration of the intended stay.
Belgian diplomatic and consular  posts may issue a C visa (maximum 90 days) without consulting the Immigration  Office if the conditions for entry into the Schengen territory have been  satisfied. Issuance of the visa can take up to 60 days. [see article 23 of the  Community Code on Visas].
If the conditions for entry are not  met, or if the applicant is in a particular situation, the posts are required  to forward the visa application to the Immigration Office. In this case, the  processing time is longer, especially at certain times of the year (summer,  school holidays, end of the year).
Currently, the processing time can take up to 85  days from the day the Immigration Office receives the visa application.

Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under  this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
 Please click on Do  I need a visa? (https://sif-gid.ibz.be/FR/les_pays_tiers_dont_les_ressortissants_sont_soumis_a_l_obligation_de_visa.aspx) To find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the interactive  map (https://ec.europa.eu/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en) to learn more about the Schengen area.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under  this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
 Please click on Do  I need a visa? (https://sif-gid.ibz.be/FR/les_pays_tiers_dont_les_ressortissants_sont_soumis_a_l_obligation_de_visa.aspx) To find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the interactive  map (https://ec.europa.eu/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en) to learn more about the Schengen area.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Business Visa]
Please note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange       fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of Euro 31 per application over and above visa fees (all applicable taxes included).
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
All fees are non-refundable.  

## Documents Required
Please click here for the checklist for Business 

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new  photo format, as explained below:

General
 The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO  (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use       plain light-coloured background.
- The       photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not       tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close       up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%       of the photograph.
- Photo       in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size 
 The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The       picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A       smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels       by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On       the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x       height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid,       but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally,       the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An       image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used       to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The       only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format).       The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected       (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If  children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport  of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued  to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they  traveling with.
 Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph  presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered  incomplete.

## Processing Time
Processing time of a short stay visa  depends on the purpose and duration of the intended stay.
Belgian diplomatic and consular  posts may issue a C visa (maximum 90 days) without consulting the Immigration  Office if the conditions for entry into the Schengen territory have been  satisfied. Issuance of the visa can take up to 60 days. [see article 23 of the  Community Code on Visas].
If the conditions for entry are not  met, or if the applicant is in a particular situation, the posts are required  to forward the visa application to the Immigration Office. In this case, the  processing time is longer, especially at certain times of the year (summer,  school holidays, end of the year).
Currently, the processing time can take up to 85  days from the day the Immigration Office receives the visa application.

## Online Application Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) to fill the online Visa form.

## Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under  this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
 Please click on Do  I need a visa? (https://sif-gid.ibz.be/FR/les_pays_tiers_dont_les_ressortissants_sont_soumis_a_l_obligation_de_visa.aspx) To find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the  interactive  map (https://ec.europa.eu/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en) to learn more about the Schengen area.

## Visa Fees

[bel > irn > en > table1 > Cohabitation Visa]

[Table:  Study Or Study Related Stay]

[Table:  Study Or Study Related Stay]
 Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of Euro 31 per application over and above visa fees (all applicable taxes included).
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please click here for Cohabitation checklist

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new  photo format, as explained below:

General
 The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO  (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use       plain light-coloured background.
- The       photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not       tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close       up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%       of the photograph.
- Photo       in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size 
 The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The       picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A       smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels       by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On       the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x       height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid,       but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally,       the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An       image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used       to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The       only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format).       The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected       (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If  children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport  of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued  to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they  traveling with.
 Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph  presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered  incomplete.

## Processing Time
Processing time of a short stay visa  depends on the purpose and duration of the intended stay.
Belgian diplomatic and consular  posts may issue a C visa (maximum 90 days) without consulting the Immigration  Office if the conditions for entry into the Schengen territory have been  satisfied. Issuance of the visa can take up to 60 days. [see article 23 of the  Community Code on Visas].
If the conditions for entry are not  met, or if the applicant is in a particular situation, the posts are required  to forward the visa application to the Immigration Office. In this case, the  processing time is longer, especially at certain times of the year (summer,  school holidays, end of the year).
Currently, the processing time can take up to 85  days from the day the Immigration Office receives the visa application.

## Online Application Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) to fill the online Visa form.

- The       photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not       tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close       up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%       of the photograph.
- Photo       in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size 
 The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The       picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A       smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels       by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On       the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x       height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid,       but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally,       the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An       image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used       to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The       only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format).       The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected       (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If  children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport  of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued  to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they  traveling with.
 Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph  presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered  incomplete.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Sport Cultural]
Please note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange       fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of Euro 31 per application over and above visa fees (all applicable taxes included).
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
Processing time of a short stay visa  depends on the purpose and duration of the intended stay.
Belgian diplomatic and consular  posts may issue a C visa (maximum 90 days) without consulting the Immigration  Office if the conditions for entry into the Schengen territory have been  satisfied. Issuance of the visa can take up to 60 days. [see article 23 of the  Community Code on Visas].
If the conditions for entry are not  met, or if the applicant is in a particular situation, the posts are required  to forward the visa application to the Immigration Office. In this case, the  processing time is longer, especially at certain times of the year (summer,  school holidays, end of the year).
Currently, the processing time can take up to 85  days from the day the Immigration Office receives the visa application.

## Photo Specifications

General

- Use       plain light-coloured background.
- The       photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not       tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close       up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%       of the photograph.
- Photo       in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size 
 The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The       picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A       smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels       by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On       the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x       height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid,       but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally,       the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An       image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used       to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The       only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format).       The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected       (a warning will be shown).

- Glue       one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If  children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport  of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued  to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they  traveling with.
 Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph  presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered  incomplete.

## Processing Time

## Processing Time
Processing time of a short stay visa  depends on the purpose and duration of the intended stay.
Belgian diplomatic and consular  posts may issue a C visa (maximum 90 days) without consulting the Immigration  Office if the conditions for entry into the Schengen territory have been  satisfied. Issuance of the visa can take up to 60 days. [see article 23 of the  Community Code on Visas].
If the conditions for entry are not  met, or if the applicant is in a particular situation, the posts are required  to forward the visa application to the Immigration Office. In this case, the  processing time is longer, especially at certain times of the year (summer,  school holidays, end of the year).
Currently, the processing time can take up to 85  days from the day the Immigration Office receives the visa application.

- Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) to fill the online Visa form.
